metadata.title: CAPS Physical Sciences Grade 12 (Matric)
metadata.notes: Curated from CAPS Physical Sciences FET Phase (DBE, 2011 amended 2022). NSC Matric examination syllabus — Paper 1 Physics (150 marks, 3 hours) and Paper 2 Chemistry (150 marks, 3 hours).

nodes.title: Mechanics — Momentum and Impulse
nodes.description: Conservation of linear momentum (Σpi = Σpf). Impulse (FnetΔt = mΔv = Δp). Elastic and inelastic collisions in one dimension. Newton's second law in terms of momentum. Application to safety: crumple zones, airbags, seatbelts

academic_standards.description: Conservation of linear momentum (Σpi = Σpf). Impulse (FnetΔt = mΔv = Δp). Elastic and inelastic collisions in one dimension. Newton's second law in terms of momentum. Application to safety: crumple zones, airbags, seatbelts

supplementary_resources.title: Khan Academy: Mechanics — Momentum and Impulse

nodes.title: Mechanics — Vertical Projectile Motion
nodes.description: Motion in one dimension under gravity. Equations of motion with g = 9.8 m/s². Free-fall, bouncing ball, projectile launched vertically. Graphs of motion (x-t, v-t). Reaction time experiments

academic_standards.description: Motion in one dimension under gravity. Equations of motion with g = 9.8 m/s². Free-fall, bouncing ball, projectile launched vertically. Graphs of motion (x-t, v-t). Reaction time experiments

supplementary_resources.title: Khan Academy: Mechanics — Vertical Projectile Motion

nodes.title: Mechanics — Work, Energy, and Power
nodes.description: Work (W = FΔx cos θ, positive/negative/zero work). Work-energy theorem (Wnet = ΔEk). Conservation of energy including non-conservative forces (Wnc = ΔEk + ΔEp). Power (P = W/Δt = Fv). Efficiency

academic_standards.description: Work (W = FΔx cos θ, positive/negative/zero work). Work-energy theorem (Wnet = ΔEk). Conservation of energy including non-conservative forces (Wnc = ΔEk + ΔEp). Power (P = W/Δt = Fv). Efficiency

supplementary_resources.title: Khan Academy: Mechanics — Work, Energy, and Power
YouTube: Mechanics — Work, Energy, and Power
Wikipedia: Mechanics — Work, Energy, and Power

nodes.title: Waves — Doppler Effect
nodes.description: The Doppler effect with sound. Observer frequency formula: fL = fs × (v ± vL)/(v ± vs). Applications: radar, medical ultrasound, astronomy (red shift). Supersonic motion and shock waves (Mach cone)

academic_standards.description: The Doppler effect with sound. Observer frequency formula: fL = fs × (v ± vL)/(v ± vs). Applications: radar, medical ultrasound, astronomy (red shift). Supersonic motion and shock waves (Mach cone)

nodes.title: Electricity — Electric Circuits
nodes.description: Internal resistance and EMF (ε = I(R + r)). Series-parallel networks. Power dissipation. Kirchhoff's voltage and current laws. Wheatstone bridge (enrichment). Motor effect

academic_standards.description: Internal resistance and EMF (ε = I(R + r)). Series-parallel networks. Power dissipation. Kirchhoff's voltage and current laws. Wheatstone bridge (enrichment). Motor effect

nodes.title: Electricity — Electrodynamics
nodes.description: Generators (AC and DC principles). Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction (ε = −NΔΦ/Δt). Motors (DC motor operation). Alternating current: Vrms, Irms, Pavg. Transformers (Vs/Vp = Ns/Np). National electricity grid

academic_standards.description: Generators (AC and DC principles). Faraday's law of electromagnetic induction (ε = −NΔΦ/Δt). Motors (DC motor operation). Alternating current: Vrms, Irms, Pavg. Transformers (Vs/Vp = Ns/Np). National electricity grid

nodes.title: Modern Physics — Photoelectric Effect
nodes.description: Dual nature of light. Photoelectric effect: threshold frequency (f₀), work function (W₀ = hf₀), Einstein's equation (E = W₀ + Ekmax = hf). Particle nature of light. Photon energy (E = hf = hc/λ). Photocell applications

academic_standards.description: Dual nature of light. Photoelectric effect: threshold frequency (f₀), work function (W₀ = hf₀), Einstein's equation (E = W₀ + Ekmax = hf). Particle nature of light. Photon energy (E = hf = hc/λ). Photocell applications

supplementary_resources.title: Khan Academy: Modern Physics — Photoelectric Effect

nodes.title: Modern Physics — Emission and Absorption Spectra
nodes.description: Atomic energy levels. Emission spectra (continuous, line/bright). Absorption spectra (dark lines). Bohr model. E = hf relationship for photon emission/absorption. Applications: stellar composition, flame tests

academic_standards.description: Atomic energy levels. Emission spectra (continuous, line/bright). Absorption spectra (dark lines). Bohr model. E = hf relationship for photon emission/absorption. Applications: stellar composition, flame tests

supplementary_resources.title: Khan Academy: Modern Physics — Emission and Absorption Spectra
YouTube: Modern Physics — Emission and Absorption Spectra
Wikipedia: Modern Physics — Emission and Absorption Spectra

nodes.title: Organic Chemistry — Nomenclature and Reactions
nodes.description: IUPAC naming: alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, alcohols (primary/secondary/tertiary), carboxylic acids, esters, aldehydes, ketones, amines, amides, alkyl halides. Structural, condensed, and molecular formulae. Functional group identification. Isomers (structural, functional group, positional, chain)

academic_standards.description: IUPAC naming: alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, alcohols (primary/secondary/tertiary), carboxylic acids, esters, aldehydes, ketones, amines, amides, alkyl halides. Structural, condensed, and molecular formulae. Functional group identification. Isomers (structural, functional group, positional, chain)

supplementary_resources.title: Khan Academy: Organic Chemistry — Nomenclature and Reactions
YouTube: Organic Chemistry — Nomenclature and Reactions
Wikipedia: Organic Chemistry — Nomenclature and Reactions

nodes.title: Organic Chemistry — Reactions and Applications
nodes.description: Substitution (haloalkanes + OH⁻). Elimination (dehydrohalogenation). Addition (alkenes: HX, X₂, H₂O, H₂). Esterification and hydrolysis. Combustion. Oxidation of alcohols. Polymerisation (addition and condensation). Plastics and polymers. Petrochemicals and the refining process (fractional distillation, cracking)

academic_standards.description: Substitution (haloalkanes + OH⁻). Elimination (dehydrohalogenation). Addition (alkenes: HX, X₂, H₂O, H₂). Esterification and hydrolysis. Combustion. Oxidation of alcohols. Polymerisation (addition and condensation). Plastics and polymers. Petrochemicals and the refining process (fractional distillation, cracking)

supplementary_resources.title: Khan Academy: Organic Chemistry — Reactions and Applications
YouTube: Organic Chemistry — Reactions and Applications
Wikipedia: Organic Chemistry — Reactions and Applications

nodes.title: Chemical Change — Reaction Rates
nodes.description: Qualitative and quantitative rate. Rate = −Δ[reactant]/Δt = +Δ[product]/Δt. Factors: concentration, temperature, surface area, catalysts. Collision theory. Activation energy and energy profiles. Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution. Mechanism of catalysis

academic_standards.description: Qualitative and quantitative rate. Rate = −Δ[reactant]/Δt = +Δ[product]/Δt. Factors: concentration, temperature, surface area, catalysts. Collision theory. Activation energy and energy profiles. Maxwell-Boltzmann distribution. Mechanism of catalysis

supplementary_resources.title: Khan Academy: Chemical Change — Reaction Rates

nodes.title: Chemical Equilibrium
nodes.description: Reversible reactions and dynamic equilibrium. Le Chatelier's principle applied to concentration, temperature, pressure changes. Equilibrium constant Kc and its interpretation. Effect of catalyst on equilibrium (no shift, faster attainment). Industrial applications: Haber process, Contact process

academic_standards.description: Reversible reactions and dynamic equilibrium. Le Chatelier's principle applied to concentration, temperature, pressure changes. Equilibrium constant Kc and its interpretation. Effect of catalyst on equilibrium (no shift, faster attainment). Industrial applications: Haber process, Contact process

nodes.title: Acids and Bases
nodes.description: Brønsted-Lowry model. Conjugate acid-base pairs. Strong vs weak acids/bases (degree of ionisation). Ka, Kb, Kw. pH calculations: pH = −log[H₃O⁺]. Titration curves (strong/strong, strong/weak). Indicators and equivalence point. Hydrolysis of salts. Buffer solutions (conceptual)

academic_standards.description: Brønsted-Lowry model. Conjugate acid-base pairs. Strong vs weak acids/bases (degree of ionisation). Ka, Kb, Kw. pH calculations: pH = −log[H₃O⁺]. Titration curves (strong/strong, strong/weak). Indicators and equivalence point. Hydrolysis of salts. Buffer solutions (conceptual)

nodes.title: Electrochemistry — Galvanic and Electrolytic Cells
nodes.description: Galvanic cells: spontaneous redox reactions, cell notation, standard hydrogen electrode, EMF calculation (E°cell = E°cathode − E°anode). Electrolytic cells: non-spontaneous, external voltage, anode/cathode identification. Faraday's laws. Applications: electroplating, refining of copper, chlor-alkali process. Comparison of galvanic and electrolytic cells

academic_standards.description: Galvanic cells: spontaneous redox reactions, cell notation, standard hydrogen electrode, EMF calculation (E°cell = E°cathode − E°anode). Electrolytic cells: non-spontaneous, external voltage, anode/cathode identification. Faraday's laws. Applications: electroplating, refining of copper, chlor-alkali process. Comparison of galvanic and electrolytic cells

supplementary_resources.title: Khan Academy: Electrochemistry — Galvanic and Electrolytic ...

nodes.title: Chemical Industry — Fertilisers
nodes.description: N, P, K in fertilisers. Haber process (NH₃). Ostwald process (HNO₃). Contact process (H₂SO₄). Superphosphate production. NPK ratios. Environmental impact: eutrophication, soil acidification

academic_standards.description: N, P, K in fertilisers. Haber process (NH₃). Ostwald process (HNO₃). Contact process (H₂SO₄). Superphosphate production. NPK ratios. Environmental impact: eutrophication, soil acidification

edges.rationale: Momentum concepts before projectile motion applications
Equilibrium needed for industrial processes (Haber, Contact)
Grade 11 optics/waves before Grade 12 Doppler effect
Grade 11 organic introduction before Grade 12 organic chemistry
Grade 11 reaction rates before Grade 12 rates
Grade 11 acids and bases before Grade 12 acids and bases
